agent.root_goal: Build a full-stack todo app: design schema, implement backend API, implement frontend, write integration tests.
agent.task: Build a full-stack todo app: design schema, implement backend API, implement frontend, write integration tests.

action.reasoning: The root task is to build a full-stack todo app, which involves distinct phases: schema design, backend implementation, frontend implementation, and integration testing. These phases have clear dependencies (backend depends on schema, frontend depends on backend, tests depend on both). I will decompose this into four subtasks. The schema design is the first step. The backend and frontend can be developed in parallel once the schema is defined. The integration tests will run last, after both frontend and backend are complete. I will take the self_role of orchestrating the final assembly and ensuring the project structure is coherent.

subtasks.goal: Design the database schema and data models for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask defines the core data structures (Todo, User, etc.) and relationships. It should output a schema definition file (e.g., SQL or Prisma schema) and a brief documentation of the models. This is the foundation for the backend and frontend.

outputs.description: The database schema definition file (e.g., schema.prisma or schema.sql).
Brief documentation explaining the data models and relationships.

subtasks.goal: Implement the backend API for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask implements the server-side logic, including RESTful endpoints for CRUD operations on todos. It should use the schema defined in root.1. It should output the backend code and instructions for running the server.

outputs.description: API specification (e.g., OpenAPI/Swagger) or endpoint documentation.

subtasks.goal: Implement the frontend UI for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask implements the client-side interface, allowing users to create, read, update, and delete todos. It should interact with the backend API defined in root.2. It should output the frontend code and instructions for running the client.

outputs.description: Instructions for setting up and running the frontend.

subtasks.goal: Write integration tests for the full-stack todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask writes tests that verify the interaction between the frontend and backend. It should test the full CRUD lifecycle. It depends on both the backend and frontend being implemented.

outputs.description: A summary of the tests and how to run them.

self_role.goal: Assemble the final project structure and documentation.

outputs.description: A final README or project structure document that ties together the schema, backend, frontend, and tests.

self_role.condition: completed("root.1") AND completed("root.2") AND completed("root.3") AND completed("root.4")

action.reasoning: The schema is available and defines the data models (User, Todo, Tag). I can now implement the backend API. The task is substantial but fits within a single focused effort if I use a standard framework like Express with Prisma. I will generate the server code, route handlers for CRUD operations, and the API specification. I will fulfill both assigned pins: backend_code and api_spec.

result_outputs.description: Complete backend source code including server setup, Prisma client initialization, and RESTful route handlers for User, Todo, and Tag CRUD operations.
OpenAPI 3.0 specification documenting all endpoints, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements.

action.reasoning: The API spec is available and defines all necessary endpoints for authentication and todo CRUD operations. The frontend task is substantial but can be completed in a single focused effort using a standard stack like React with Vite. I will implement the complete frontend code and README myself, as spawning subtasks for a single-page app would add unnecessary overhead. The frontend will include login/register pages, a todo list view, and forms for creating/editing todos, interacting with the backend at localhost:3000/api.

result_outputs.description: The complete frontend source code for the todo app, including React components, API service layer, and styling.
Instructions for setting up and running the frontend, including dependencies and environment configuration.

action.reasoning: The backend code is fully available and the frontend code exists (though truncated, the backend API contract is clear). I can write comprehensive integration tests for the backend API endpoints (auth and todos) using supertest and Jest. These tests will cover the full CRUD lifecycle, authentication flows, and error handling. Since the backend is a Node.js/Express app with Prisma, I will use a test database setup. I will produce the test suite code and a test report explaining how to run them.

result_outputs.description: Integration test suite for the todo app backend API using Jest and Supertest.
Summary of the integration tests, coverage areas, and instructions on how to run them.
